heading: Multifamily Housing
relation: A TOD approach to density and mix of uses prioritizes communities with less multifamily housing.
justification: Multifamily housing refers to residential properties that contain multiple separate housing units, allowing more than one family to live independently within the same building or complex. TOD prioritizes building multifamily housing near transit hubs to increase affordability.

heading: Mixed-use Development
relation: A TOD approach to density and mix of uses prioritizes communities with less mixed-use development.
justification: Mixed-use development entertains multiple use purposes (commercial industries, residential properties, recreation, etc.) underneath a single parcel of land, aiming to let micro-mobility reach multiple types of facilities.

heading: Rent Burden
relation: A TOD approach to equitable development prioritizes communities with more rent burden.
justification: Determined by the U.S. Congress, rent burden includes individuals who dedicate 30% of their monthly income to household fees (i.e rent, electrical, water). Affordable housing for low-income and/or rent burdened persons near transit stations encourages accessibility for longer travel distances.

heading: Resistance to Climate Change
relation: A TOD approach to a great public realm prioritizes communities with higher resistance to climate change.
justification: Established by the MAPC, measures an area's adaptability to lower air quality, hotter temperatures and natural disasters. Guaranteeing safety and accessibility in areas of low adaptability to heat is important, especially for movement within a walkshed.

heading: Disability
relation: A TOD approach to a greater public realm prioritizes communities with a higher percentage of disabled persons.
justification: Transit oriented development prioritizes accessibility and mobility. In Massachusetts, all walking environments must provide full Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA)-compliant accessibility for all pedestrians with disabilities.

heading: Parking Lot Area Nearby
relation: A TOD approach to parking prioritizes transit stops with a low amount of parking facilities.
justification: Park and Ride is a transit spot to provide vehicle parking near public transport hubs. This aims to reduce the number of private vehicles in urban areas, thus producing flow to public ridership.
